pages.clients.cardNewClientHint: After saving, account status and live connection will appear here.
pages.clients.confirmDelete: Delete this client? This cannot be undone.
pages.clients.editInboundsHint: Changing inbounds replaces all assignments for this client.
pages.clients.keyQrButtonTitle: Show QR code for this connection string
pages.clients.keyQrTooLong: Text is too long for a QR code. Use copy, or open on a device with a smaller key.
pages.clients.keysTelemtHint: Telemt MTProto: open the tg://proxy link in Telegram (or copy the proxy URL from your subscription). Secrets are tied to this client on this inbound.
pages.clients.keysWireGuardHint: WireGuard does not use a v2ray:// link. The block is server + setup notes. In the inbound’s WireGuard peers, add each device’s public key; set optional email on a peer to match this client and show that row in this card.
pages.clients.sessions.mtprotoBlockHint: MTProto (Telemt): Block uses the same switch as for Xray — the IP is added to the session blocklist (subscription requests from that IP are denied). Xray routing rules also apply where this client uses Xray. For MTProto traffic, the panel additionally tries to drop active TCP for that IP (conntrack on the panel host or worker nodes), same idea as Disconnect.
pages.clients.subIdHint: Optional. If empty, a random ID is generated.
pages.inbounds.addInboundNodesEmpty: No nodes configured yet. Inbound will run on the main server.
pages.inbounds.hysteriaTlsHint: QUIC uses TLS like 3x-ui: SNI, ALPN (usually h3), certificate paths (optional — panel default cert is applied if empty).
pages.inbounds.mixedAuthHint: First saved account in settings; panel clients map email local-part to this user when syncing to Xray.
pages.inbounds.mixedStreamHint: Mixed serves HTTP and SOCKS on one port — plain TCP only. TLS/REALITY and WebSocket do not apply here.
pages.inbounds.sectionAuthNone: No protocol-specific secrets on this screen (e.g. VMess uses client UUIDs in settings).
pages.inbounds.shadowsocksStreamHint: Shadowsocks already encrypts payload. The stream is plain TCP or WebSocket (no TLS/REALITY here — that applies to VLESS, VMess, Trojan).
pages.inbounds.stepTelemtAuthDesc: Fake-TLS modes, SNI domain, links, API
pages.inbounds.streamSecurityNoneForSs: Stream security is none (Xray default for this protocol).
pages.inbounds.telemtMetricsPortHint: [server] Prometheus/HTTP metrics listener port; leave empty to omit.
pages.inbounds.telemtModesExplain: tls = Fake-TLS (Telegram `ee…` links). secure = `dd…` prefix. classic = plain MTProto secret. Official reference: github.com/telemt/telemt — config.toml & docs.
pages.inbounds.telemtSettingsHint: Open this wizard step from the left: «Telemt (MTProto)». Here you configure Telemt [general], [general.modes] (Fake-TLS = tls), [censorship] SNI/masking, links and API. Client MTProto secrets are created when users are assigned to this inbound.
pages.inbounds.telemtTlsDomainHint: Fake-TLS fronting hostname written to Telemt [censorship] tls_domain. Optional JSON key censorship.sni is accepted as an alias when importing configs.
pages.inbounds.telemtTomlPreviewHint: Generated Telemt config.toml (same as deployed to the node or local data/telemt on standalone). [access.users] is empty until you save the inbound and assign clients; after that it reflects the database.
pages.inbounds.telemtTransportHint: Telemt does not use Xray `streamSettings`. The process listens on this inbound port with Telemt-specific options on the next step; users and MTProto secrets are created when clients are assigned.
pages.inbounds.telemtUnknownSniActionHin: Telemt [censorship]: how to treat an SNI mismatch. Default (empty) follows Telemt defaults. mask / reject_handshake when you need explicit policy.
pages.inbounds.tlsCertHint: Use certificate file + key file on the server, or paste PEM blocks below.
pages.inbounds.xhttpHint: XHTTP (SplitHTTP) — same fields as 3x-ui / Xray xhttpSettings (request headers, mode, padding, session, uplink).
pages.inbounds.xrayCorePreviewHint: Single inbound object as it is merged into the Xray core config (listen, port, tag, protocol, settings, streamSettings, sniffing). Panel API request format is not shown here.
pages.settings.dockerUpdaterProgressHint: Please wait while update is being applied.
pages.xray.resetServerTemplateConfirm: Reset the server routing template to the default? Changes apply after you save the Xray config.
pages.xray.simpleApiSectionHint: Handler / Logger / Stats must match the dokodemo-door inbound tag used for the panel.
pages.xray.simpleApiTagDesc: Same as the API inbound `tag` (e.g. api).
pages.xray.simplePolicySectionHint: Toggles for common `policy.levels.0` and `policy.system` stat switches.
subBuilder.customRemarks.hwidUnsupported: HWID: no device id (not used yet)
subBuilder.customRemarks.intro: When a client cannot receive real nodes (expired, limited, disabled, HWID limit, or no inbounds), the server can return dummy subscription lines whose titles are these texts. One line per remark; empty hosts messages are always used when there are no inbounds.
subBuilder.customRemarks.lineHint: One line per item shown in the client as a node title.
subBuilder.customRemarks.title: Custom subscription remarks
subBuilder.install.useEncryptedDesc: Generate happ://crypt4 / v2raytun://crypt via the server when available.
subBuilder.responseRules.mtProtoToggleHi: When the subscription includes Telegram MTProto (Telemt) links, show a helper next to the installation guide with tg:// open, QR, and copy — same idea as connection keys.
